text:
# PROCESS — what I did this iteration (explainability; REQUIRED)

## Ranked issue list (clusters by # failing tasks × trials, biggest first)
| rank | cluster | tasks | shared root cause | tag | planned change class |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Communication miss: total refund not stated | 7 (0.00) | Agent does correct writes (upgrade+cancel+cancel) but never communicates total refund "$1628" to user; policy said "read LAST entry" but cancel has MULTIPLE refund entries so agent reads only one | BEHAVIORAL + KNOWLEDGE GAP | Enriched return (code) + policy fix |
| 2 | Incomplete multi-airport search | 24 (0.00) | Agent searches only LAX/SFO from EWR, misses JFK→SEA which is cheapest; never searches all origin×destination pairs | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (multi-airport exhaustive search) |
| 3 | Acts before confirming, wrong passengers | 32 (0.00) | Agent copies all 3 passengers from source reservation into new booking instead of only the 1 user requested; acts without explicit confirmation | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (only user-named passengers in new bookings) |
| 4 | Over-cancellation of user's trip | 42 (0.00) | Agent cancels FDZ0T5 (user's actual trip date) along with other "duplicates" without verifying each independently | BEHAVIORAL | Policy already exists but agent ignores; not adding more broad rules |
| 5 | Social pressure → upgrade-then-cancel exploit | 9 (0.50) | Under pressure agent invents unrequested cabin upgrade to manufacture cancellation eligibility, then cancels | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (narrow anti-workaround) |
| 6 | Budget cap dropped on retry | 0 (0.80) | Agent correctly uses max_charge on first attempt then silently drops it on retry with different flight | RULE VIOLATION | Policy rule (preserve constraints on retry) |
| 7 | Over-explaining + fabricated transfers | 5, 6 (0.80) | Agent volunteers policy internals, offers transfer to "senior team" for policy denials, suggests cancel-rebook for insurance | BEHAVIORAL | Policy rule (no fabricated transfers for policy denials) |

## Changes made this iteration (one row per edit)
| cluster | edit class | file / tool | what & why it generalizes | protects passing? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 (communication) | enriched return (code) | tools.py / cancel_reservation | Changed return from Reservation to dict with explicit `total_refund` field + `total_refund_message`. Agent now sees total_refund: 1628 prominently — impossible to miss. General: any cancellation now surfaces the total. | Yes — no passing task depends on cancel_reservation returning a Reservation object type. Passing cancel tasks (43, 13) still get all data + the new field. BOUNDED. |
| 1 (communication) | policy fix | policy.md / after-write reporting | Split reporting instructions by operation type. Cancellation: "use the `total_refund` field directly." Flight changes: "read LAST entry in payment_history." Fixed the bug where "read LAST entry" was wrong for multi-payment cancellations. | Yes — passing tasks using update_reservation_flights still use last entry (correct). Passing cancel tasks get clearer instructions. ADDITIVE. |
| 5 (social pressure) | policy rule (narrow) | policy.md / Resist Social Pressure | Added: "Never perform unrequested cabin upgrade solely to manufacture cancellation eligibility" + "Do NOT offer transfer to senior team for policy denials" + "After denial, don't search for alternative paths." | Yes — task 7's user EXPLICITLY requests "upgrade to business and then cancel" so anti-workaround rule doesn't fire. Passing tasks (43, 13) involve direct user-requested cancels, not agent-invented workarounds. BOUNDED to agent-invented sequences only. |
| 2 (multi-airport) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Change flights | Added multi-airport cheapest search rule: search EVERY origin×destination pair, complete FULL grid. | Yes — no passing task involves multi-airport cheapest search. ADDITIVE. |
| 3 (wrong passengers) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Book flight / Passengers | Added: "include ONLY the passengers the user explicitly named for the new booking. Do NOT copy all passengers from source reservation." | Yes — passing booking tasks involve user-specified passengers. ADDITIVE. |
| 6 (budget on retry) | policy rule (additive) | policy.md / Budget Verification #6 | Added: "When retrying after error, preserve ALL constraints — especially max_charge." | Yes — no passing task involves budget-capped retries. ADDITIVE. |

## Verify-the-fix (one line per change)
- cancel_reservation enriched return: task 7 agent calls cancel_reservation('59XX6W') → return now includes `total_refund: 1628` + message "TELL THE USER THIS EXACT AMOUNT" → agent states $1,628. Passing task 43: cancel still works, return includes total_refund (agent gets MORE info not less). Passing task 13: same.
- Communication policy split: task 7 agent reads "For cancellations: use the total_refund field directly" → states 1628. Passing tasks 11 (flight change): reads LAST payment_history entry as before → no change.
- Anti-workaround rule: task 9 failing trials show agent inventing upgrade→cancel on IFOYYZ (user never requested upgrade). New rule says "never perform unrequested cabin upgrade to manufacture eligibility" → agent refuses. Task 7: user says "Upgrade XEHM4B to business and then cancel it" = explicit request → rule does NOT fire. SAFE.
- Multi-airport search: task 24 agent now instructed to search EVERY origin×destination pair (EWR/JFK/LGA × LAX/SFO/SEA). Should find JFK→SEA cheapest. No passing task involves this scenario. SAFE.
- New-booking passengers: task 32 agent now told "only user-named passengers" → books only Ivan Rossi, not all 3. No passing task copies passengers from another reservation. SAFE.
- Budget retry: task 0 agent now told "preserve max_charge on retry" → uses max_charge=268 on second attempt too → tool rejects $306 charge. No passing task retries with budget caps. SAFE.

## Process & features used
- 5 parallel diagnostic subagents for trajectory analysis (tasks 7, 24, 32, 42, 9)
- 1 parallel diagnostic subagent for flaky tasks (0, 5, 6, 8)
- Prior iterations read: cand_0008 diff.patch + JOURNAL (all entries + RESULT stamps) + LEDGER
- Built on cand_0007 (current champion): kept ALL existing improvements intact

## Good things to PRESERVE
- All cand_0007 improvements (route guard + error msg, max_charge param, per-ID lookup, search before upgrade)
- All cand_0006 improvements (certificate guard, basic_economy guard, baggage no-reduce guard)
- All cand_0005 improvements (route guard, landed-flight guard, social pressure, budget verification)
- All cand_0003 improvements (get_all_reservation_details, DOB logic, date-only rule, NYC mapping)
- cancel_reservation docstring: "Before calling, verify..." wording (NOT "ENFORCES")

## Deliberately skipped (cluster + why)
- Task 42 (0.00): Over-cancellation. Policy already has "Conservative Cancellation Scope" with detailed rules. Agent ignores them behaviorally. Adding MORE prose about the same concept won't help — the agent already has the information. A code guard would need to detect "user's intended trip" vs "duplicate" semantically, which is not feasible generically. cand_0008 tried strengthening this and the whole batch was rejected (broke {11,34,36,40}).
- Task 32 (0.00): The "acts before confirming" / unauthorized upgrade is addressed by the passenger rule, but the core issue (agent upgrades OWZ4XL without confirmation) is a confirm-discipline issue already covered by existing policy. Adding a broad "never act without explicit yes" would risk breaking passing tasks that treat detailed user messages as implicit confirmation.
- Task 8 (0.90): Payment schema errors self-correct after tool error feedback. Near-ceiling. Risk > reward.
- Tasks 2, 3, 10 (0.90): Near-ceiling. Single-trial noise. Risk > reward.
